text: The Great War had been the explosion of a vast accumulation of energy, moral and social as well as material. Europe might, and probably would, bicker, murder, bomb, massacre, and starve, but for another generation at least she would not have either the spirit or the discipline or the material to produce such munitions and such wide-sweeping concerted action as devastated her in the Great War. She is morally and physically bankrupt and prostrate. She may go on sinking, as Asia Minor sank, back even to barbarism. Even if she does not do so, it will take forty or fifty years to reassemble energy for another such world-wide outbreak.
author: H G Wells

didItHoldUp.analysis: Wells gave Europe at least a generation before it could wage another world war. He was off by exactly one generation — World War II erupted just 15 years later, not 40 or 50. His reasoning was sound: Europe was devastated, bankrupt, and exhausted after WWI. But he underestimated how quickly industrial nations can rearm when driven by ideology, grievance, and totalitarian mobilization. Germany went from prostrate to the most powerful military in Europe in under a decade. Wells saw the exhaustion clearly but missed the fury that would fuel the recovery. The 'spirit and discipline' he thought were spent turned out to be renewable resources.
